summary:
Claim: The HCO₃⁻/H₂CO₃ buffer is optimal because its pKa closely matches physiological pH.  
Evidence: - Its pKa (~6.4) is nearest among common buffers to human blood pH (~7.4), allowing effective resistance to pH shifts. - This proximity enables maximal buffering capacity when small amounts of acid or base are introduced.  
Answer: A
